{"command":"Get-Help Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -Full","explanation":"Get full help for the interactive login app registration cmdlet","goal":"Understand app registration options","mode":"sync"}

NAME
    Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin
    
SYNOPSIS
    Registers an Entra ID App for use with Interactive login
    
    
SYNTAX
    Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -ApplicationName <String> 
    [-AzureEnvironment <AzureEnvironment>] [-DeviceLogin <SwitchParameter>] 
    [-EntraIDLoginEndPoint <String>] [-LogoFilePath <String>] 
    [-MicrosoftGraphEndPoint <String>] -Tenant <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    Register-PnPEntraIDAppForInteractiveLogin -ApplicationName <String> 
    [-AzureEnvironment <AzureEnvironment>] [-DeviceLogin <SwitchParameter>] 
    [-EntraIDLoginEndPoint <String>] [-LogoFilePath <String>] 
    [-MicrosoftGraphEndPoint <String>] -Tenant <String> [<CommonParameters>]
    
    
DESCRIPTION
    Registers an Entra ID App for use with the interactive login on 
    Connect-PnPOnline. By default it assumes an Interactive login, but you can 
    decide to use Device Login auth by specifying -DeviceLogin.
    

PARAMETERS
    -ApplicationName <String>
        The name of the Entra ID Application to create.
        
        Required?                    true
        Position?                    Named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Aliases                      none
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -AzureEnvironment [<AzureEnvironment>]
        The Azure environment to use for authentication, the defaults to 
        'Production' which is the main Azure environment.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    Named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Aliases                      none
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -DeviceLogin [<SwitchParameter>]
        If specified, a device login flow, supporting Multi-Factor 
        Authentication will be used to authenticate towards the Microsoft 
        Graph.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    Named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Aliases                      none
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -EntraIDLoginEndPoint [<String>]
        Sets the EntraID login endpoint to be used for creation of the app. 
        This only works if Azure Environment parameter is set to `Custom`
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    Named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Aliases                      none
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -GraphApplicationPermissions [<Permission[]>]
        Specify which Microsoft Graph Application permissions to request.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    0
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Aliases                      none
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -GraphDelegatePermissions [<Permission[]>]
        Specify which Microsoft Graph Delegate permissions to request.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    0
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Aliases                      none
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -LogoFilePath [<String>]
        Sets the logo for the Entra ID application. Provide a full path to a 
        local image file on your disk which you want to use as the logo.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    Named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Aliases                      none
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -MicrosoftGraphEndPoint [<String>]
        Sets the Microsoft Graph endpoint to be used for creation of the app. 
        This only works if Azure Environment parameter is set to `Custom`
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    Named
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Aliases                      none
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SharePointApplicationPermissions [<Permission[]>]
        Specify which Microsoft SharePoint Application permissions to request.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?                    0
        Default value                
        Accept pipeline input?       false
        Aliases                      none
        Accept wildcard characters?  false
        
    -SharePointDelegatePermissions [<Permission[]>]
        Specify which Microsoft SharePoint Delegate permissions to request.
        
        Required?                    false
        Position?             [truncated]
